+
+from functools import lru_cache
+from pydantic import Field
+from config import DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings
+
+
+class CounselingSettings(DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings):
+    """
+    Counseling Service configuration.
+
+    Inherits:
+    - DNAIBaseSettings: MongoDB, NATS, Redis, Logging
+    - AISettings: OpenAI, Cohere API configurations
+
+    Adds:
+    - External API configuration (NCBI)
+    - Feature flags
+    - Service-specific defaults
+
+    Uses MongoDB for data storage, Redis for caching, OpenAI/Cohere for AI generation
+    """
+
+    # Override service name
+    service_name: str = Field(
+        default="counseling_service",
+        description="Service name"
+    )
+
+    # LLM provider
+    llm_provider: str = Field(
+        default="openai",
+        description="LLM provider (openai, cohere, gemini)"
+    )
+
+    # External API configuration
+    ncbi_email: str = Field(
+        default="tung@gkim.vn",
+        description="NCBI E-utilities API email (required by NCBI)"
+    )
+
+    # Feature flags
+    enable_database_prompts: bool = Field(
+        default=True,
+        description="Enable database-driven prompts"
+    )
+
+
+# Singleton instance
+@lru_cache()
+def get_settings() -> CounselingSettings:
+    """
+    Get singleton settings instance for Counseling Service.
+
+    Uses @lru_cache to ensure settings are loaded once and cached.
+    """
+    return CounselingSettings()

diff --git a/apps/med_doc_service/settings.py b/apps/med_doc_service/settings.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..03439bb
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/med_doc_service/settings.py
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+
+from functools import lru_cache
+from pydantic import Field
+from config import DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings
+
+
+class MedDocSettings(DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings):
+    """
+    Medical Documentation Service configuration.
+
+    Inherits:
+    - DNAIBaseSettings: MongoDB, NATS, Redis, Logging
+    - AISettings: OpenAI, Cohere, Gemini API configurations
+
+    Adds:
+    - SOAP-specific LLM parameters
+    - Service-specific defaults
+
+    Does NOT use:
+    - MongoDB (no database)
+    - Redis (no caching)
+    - AWS (no cloud services)
+    """
+
+    # Override service name
+    service_name: str = Field(
+        default="med_doc_service",
+        description="Service name"
+    )
+
+    # SOAP generation is the primary feature - ensure defaults are set
+    soap_llm_temperature: float = Field(
+        default=0.5,
+        ge=0.0,
+        le=2.0,
+        description="LLM temperature for SOAP generation"
+    )
+
+    soap_llm_max_tokens: int = Field(
+        default=4000,
+        ge=100,
+        le=32000,
+        description="Maximum tokens for SOAP generation"
+    )
+
+    soap_max_prompt_length: int = Field(
+        default=100000,
+        ge=1000,
+        description="Maximum prompt length for SOAP generation"
+    )
+
+    soap_llm_max_retries: int = Field(
+        default=3,
+        ge=1,
+        le=10,
+        description="Maximum retries for SOAP LLM calls"
+    )
+
+    soap_llm_retry_delay: int = Field(
+        default=2,
+        ge=1,
+        le=60,
+        description="Retry delay for SOAP LLM calls (seconds)"
+    )
+
+
+# Singleton instance
+@lru_cache()
+def get_settings() -> MedDocSettings:
+    """
+    Get singleton settings instance for Med Doc Service.
+
+    Uses @lru_cache to ensure settings are loaded once and cached.
+    """
+    return MedDocSettings()

diff --git a/apps/ocr_service/settings.py b/apps/ocr_service/settings.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..afc82e3
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/ocr_service/settings.py
@@ -0,0 +1,64 @@
+
+from functools import lru_cache
+from pydantic import Field
+from config import DNAIBaseSettings, AWSSettings, AISettings
+
+
+class OCRSettings(DNAIBaseSettings, AWSSettings, AISettings):
+    """
+    OCR Service configuration.
+
+    Inherits:
+    - DNAIBaseSettings: MongoDB, NATS, Redis, Logging
+    - AWSSettings: AWS credentials for S3 file download
+    - AISettings: Gemini API for OCR processing
+
+    Adds:
+    - OCR-specific configuration
+    - Service-specific defaults
+
+    Uses S3 for file storage and Gemini for OCR processing
+    """
+
+    # Override service name
+    service_name: str = Field(
+        default="ocr_service",
+        description="Service name"
+    )
+
+    # OCR configuration
+    ocr_method: str = Field(
+        default="docling",
+        description="OCR processing method: 'docling' (on-premise) or 'gemini' (cloud API)"
+    )
+
+    ocr_max_file_size: int = Field(
+        default=10485760,  # 10MB
+        ge=1024,
+        description="Maximum OCR file size in bytes"
+    )
+
+    ocr_num_threads: int = Field(
+        default=4,
+        ge=1,
+        le=16,
+        description="Number of OCR processing threads (Docling only)"
+    )
+
+    ocr_timeout: int = Field(
+        default=180,
+        ge=30,
+        le=600,
+        description="OCR processing timeout in seconds"
+    )
+
+
+# Singleton instance
+@lru_cache()
+def get_settings() -> OCRSettings:
+    """
+    Get singleton settings instance for OCR Service.
+
+    Uses @lru_cache to ensure settings are loaded once and cached.
+    """
+    return OCRSettings()

new file mode 100644
index 0000000..9e76d7b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/config/src/config/ai_settings.py
@@ -0,0 +1,114 @@
+
+from functools import lru_cache
+from typing import Optional
+from pydantic import Field, field_validator
+from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
+from .utils import find_env_file
+
+
+class AISettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    AI/LLM configuration mixin.
+
+    Services inherit this to get AI capabilities (OpenAI, Cohere, Gemini).
+
+    Used by:
+    - Counseling Service (OpenAI + Cohere)
+    - Audio Scoring Service (OpenAI for scoring)
+    - Med Doc Service (OpenAI for SOAP generation)
+    - OCR Service (Gemini for OCR)
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
+        env_file=find_env_file(),
+        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
+        case_sensitive=False,
+        extra="ignore",
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # API KEYS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    openai_api_key: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="OpenAI API key",
+        alias="OPENAI_API_KEY"
+    )
+
+    cohere_api_key: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="Cohere API key",
+        alias="COHERE_API_KEY"
+    )
+
+    gemini_api_key: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="Google Gemini API key"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # MODEL CONFIGURATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    llm_provider: str = Field(
+        default="openai",
+        description="LLM provider (openai, cohere, gemini)"
+    )
+
+    openai_model: str = Field(
+        default="gpt-4.1",
+        description="OpenAI model name"
+    )
+
+    cohere_model: str = Field(
+        default="command-r-plus",
+        description="Cohere model name"
+    )
+
+    gemini_model: str = Field(
+        default="gemini-2.0-flash",
+        description="Gemini model name"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # LLM PARAMETERS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    llm_max_tokens: int = Field(
+        default=4096,
+        ge=1,
+        le=128000,
+        description="Maximum tokens for LLM response"
+    )
+
+    llm_temperature: float = Field(
+        default=0.7,
+        ge=0.0,
+        le=2.0,
+        description="LLM temperature (creativity)"
+    )
+
+    llm_timeout: int = Field(
+        default=120,
+        ge=10,
+        le=600,
+        description="LLM request timeout (seconds)"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # VALIDATORS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    @field_validator('llm_provider')
+    @classmethod
+    def validate_llm_provider(cls, v: str) -> str:
+        """Validate LLM provider."""
+        valid_providers = ["openai", "cohere", "gemini"]
+        if v.lower() not in valid_providers:
+            raise ValueError(f"LLM provider must be one of: {valid_providers}")
+        return v.lower()
+@lru_cache()
+def get_ai_settings() -> AISettings:
+    """Get singleton AWS settings instance."""
+    return AISettings()

new file mode 100644
index 0000000..3b59182
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/config/src/config/aws_settings.py
@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+
+from functools import lru_cache
+from typing import Optional, Dict, Any
+from pydantic import Field
+from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
+from .utils import find_env_file
+
+
+class AWSSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    AWS infrastructure configuration mixin.
+
+    Services inherit this to get AWS capabilities (S3, Transcribe, etc.).
+
+    Used by:
+    - Audio Scoring Service (S3 + Transcribe)
+    - OCR Service (S3 for file storage)
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
+        env_file=find_env_file(),
+        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
+        case_sensitive=False,
+        extra="ignore",
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # CREDENTIALS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    aws_access_key_id: str = Field(
+        default="",
+        description="AWS access key ID"
+    )
+
+    aws_secret_access_key: str = Field(
+        default="",
+        description="AWS secret access key"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # CONFIGURATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    aws_region: str = Field(
+        default="us-east-1",
+        description="AWS region"
+    )
+
+    aws_bucket_name: str = Field(
+        default="dna-insight-internal",
+        description="S3 bucket name for file storage"
+    )
+
+    aws_s3_endpoint_url: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="Custom S3 endpoint URL (for MinIO, LocalStack testing)"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # UTILITY METHODS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    def get_boto3_config(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Get boto3 client configuration.
+
+        Returns dict for boto3.client() initialization.
+        """
+        config = {
+            "aws_access_key_id": self.aws_access_key_id,
+            "aws_secret_access_key": self.aws_secret_access_key,
+            "region_name": self.aws_region,
+        }
+
+        if self.aws_s3_endpoint_url:
+            config["endpoint_url"] = self.aws_s3_endpoint_url
+
+        return config
+
+    def get_s3_client_config(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get S3-specific client configuration."""
+        return self.get_boto3_config()
+
+    def get_transcribe_client_config(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get Transcribe-specific client configuration."""
+        return self.get_boto3_config()
+
+
+@lru_cache()
+def get_aws_settings() -> AWSSettings:
+    """Get singleton AWS settings instance."""
+    return AWSSettings()
diff --git a/packages/config/src/config/base_settings.py b/packages/config/src/config/base_settings.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..7c81283
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/config/src/config/base_settings.py
@@ -0,0 +1,399 @@
+from functools import lru_cache
+from typing import Dict, Any, Optional
+from pydantic import Field, field_validator
+from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
+from .utils import find_env_file
+
+
+class DNAIBaseSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    Base settings for all DNAI microservices.
+
+    Provides core infrastructure settings:
+    - MongoDB database connection
+    - NATS messaging system
+    - Redis caching (optional)
+    - Logging and monitoring
+    - Service identification
+
+    Services inherit and add their specific needs via mixins:
+    - AISettings: for services using LLMs (OpenAI, Cohere, Gemini)
+    - AWSSettings: for services using AWS (S3, Transcribe)
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
+        env_file=find_env_file(),
+        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
+        case_sensitive=False,
+        extra="ignore",
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # SERVICE IDENTIFICATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    service_name: str = Field(
+        default="dnai_service",
+        description="Service name for identification"
+    )
+
+    environment: str = Field(
+        default="development",
+        description="Environment (development, staging, production)"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # MONGODB CONFIGURATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    mongodb_username: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="MongoDB username"
+    )
+
+    mongodb_password: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="MongoDB password"
+    )
+
+    mongodb_uri: str = Field(
+        default="mongodb://localhost:27017",
+        alias="MONGODB_URI",
+        description="MongoDB connection URI"
+    )
+
+    mongodb_database: str = Field(
+        default="dev_dnai_db",
+        alias="MONGODB_DATABASE",
+        description="MongoDB database name"
+    )
+
+    mongodb_max_pool: int = Field(
+        default=20,
+        ge=1,
+        le=100,
+        description="MongoDB maximum pool size"
+    )
+
+    mongodb_min_pool: int = Field(
+        default=5,
+        ge=1,
+        le=50,
+        description="MongoDB minimum pool size"
+    )
+
+    mongodb_max_idle_time_ms: int = Field(
+        default=20000,
+        ge=1000,
+        description="MongoDB maximum idle time in ms"
+    )
+
+    mongodb_server_selection_timeout_ms: int = Field(
+        default=5000,
+        ge=1000,
+        description="MongoDB server selection timeout in ms"
+    )
+
+    mongodb_connect_timeout_ms: int = Field(
+        default=10000,
+        ge=1000,
+        description="MongoDB connection timeout in ms"
+    )
+
+    mongodb_socket_timeout: int = Field(
+        default=20000,
+        ge=1000,
+        description="MongoDB socket timeout in ms"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # NATS CONFIGURATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    nats_url: str = Field(
+        default="nats://localhost:4222",
+        alias="NATS_URL",
+        description="NATS server URL"
+    )
+
+    nats_username: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        alias="nats_user",
+        description="NATS username"
+    )
+
+    nats_password: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        alias="nats_pass",
+        description="NATS password"
+    )
+
+    nats_token: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="NATS authentication token"
+    )
+
+    nats_stream_replicas: int = Field(
+        default=1,
+        ge=1,
+        le=5,
+        alias="NATS_REPLICAS",
+        description="Number of replicas for NATS JetStream streams"
+    )
+
+    nats_cluster_id: str = Field(
+        default="counseling-cluster",
+        description="NATS cluster ID"
+    )
+
+    nats_client_id: str = Field(
+        default="counseling_service",
+        description="NATS client ID"
+    )
+
+    stream_name: str = Field(
+        default="counseling",
+        description="JetStream stream name"
+    )
+
+    consumer_name: str = Field(
+        default="counseling-consumer",
+        description="JetStream consumer name"
+    )
+
+    max_workers: int = Field(
+        default=10,
+        ge=1,
+        le=100,
+        description="Maximum FastStream workers"
+    )
+
+    worker_timeout: int = Field(
+        default=300,
+        ge=30,
+        le=3600,
+        description="FastStream worker timeout"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # REDIS CONFIGURATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    redis_enabled: bool = Field(
+        default=False,
+        description="Enable Redis caching"
+    )
+
+    redis_host: str = Field(
+        default="localhost",
+        description="Redis host"
+    )
+
+    redis_port: int = Field(
+        default=6379,
+        ge=1024,
+        le=65535,
+        description="Redis port"
+    )
+
+    redis_db: int = Field(
+        default=0,
+        ge=0,
+        le=15,
+        description="Redis database number"
+    )
+
+    redis_username: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="Redis username (Redis 6+)"
+    )
+
+    redis_password: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="Redis password"
+    )
+
+    redis_ssl: bool = Field(
+        default=False,
+        description="Use SSL/TLS for Redis connection"
+    )
+
+    redis_cache_prefix: str = Field(
+        default="dnai:",
+        description="Redis key prefix for namespacing"
+    )
+
+    cache_ttl: int = Field(
+        default=3600,
+        ge=60,
+        description="Default cache TTL in seconds"
+    )
+    cache_max_size: int = Field(
+        default=1000,
+        description="cache_max_size"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # LOGGING CONFIGURATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    log_level: str = Field(
+        default="INFO",
+        description="Logging level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL)"
+    )
+
+    log_format: str = Field(
+        default="json",
+        description="Log format (json or text)"
+    )
+
+    log_file_path: str = Field(
+        default="",
+        description="Log file path (empty for stdout)"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # VALIDATORS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    @field_validator('mongodb_uri')
+    @classmethod
+    def validate_mongodb_uri(cls, v: str) -> str:
+        """Validate MongoDB URI format."""
+        if not v:
+            raise ValueError("MongoDB URI cannot be empty")
+        if not (v.startswith("mongodb://") or v.startswith("mongodb+srv://")):
+            raise ValueError("MongoDB URI must start with mongodb:// or mongodb+srv://")
+        return v
+
+    @field_validator('nats_url')
+    @classmethod
+    def validate_nats_url(cls, v: str) -> str:
+        """Validate NATS URL format."""
+        if not v:
+            raise ValueError("NATS URL cannot be empty")
+        if not v.startswith("nats://"):
+            raise ValueError("NATS URL must start with nats://")
+        return v
+
+    @field_validator('log_level')
+    @classmethod
+    def validate_log_level(cls, v: str) -> str:
+        """Validate log level."""
+        valid_levels = ["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL"]
+        v_upper = v.upper()
+        if v_upper not in valid_levels:
+            raise ValueError(f"Log level must be one of: {valid_levels}")
+        return v_upper
+
+    @field_validator('environment')
+    @classmethod
+    def validate_environment(cls, v: str) -> str:
+        """Validate environment."""
+        valid_envs = ["development", "staging", "production"]
+        v_lower = v.lower()
+        if v_lower not in valid_envs:
+            raise ValueError(f"Environment must be one of: {valid_envs}")
+        return v_lower
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # HELPER METHODS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    def get_mongodb_connection_string(self) -> str:
+        """Get MongoDB connection string with authentication."""
+        if self.mongodb_username and self.mongodb_password:
+            if self.mongodb_uri.startswith("mongodb://"):
+                return self.mongodb_uri.replace(
+                    "mongodb://",
+                    f"mongodb://{self.mongodb_username}:{self.mongodb_password}@"
+                )
+            elif self.mongodb_uri.startswith("mongodb+srv://"):
+                return self.mongodb_uri  # Atlas URI already includes credentials
+        return self.mongodb_uri
+
+    def get_mongodb_connection_options(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get MongoDB connection options."""
+        return {
+            "maxPoolSize": self.mongodb_max_pool,
+            "minPoolSize": self.mongodb_min_pool,
+            "maxIdleTimeMS": self.mongodb_max_idle_time_ms,
+            "serverSelectionTimeoutMS": self.mongodb_server_selection_timeout_ms,
+            "connectTimeoutMS": self.mongodb_connect_timeout_ms,
+            "socketTimeoutMS": self.mongodb_socket_timeout,
+            "retryWrites": False,
+            "retryReads": False,
+        }
+
+    def get_authenticated_nats_url(self) -> str:
+        """Get NATS URL with authentication credentials embedded."""
+        if self.nats_username and self.nats_password:
+            if "://" in self.nats_url:
+                protocol, rest = self.nats_url.split("://", 1)
+                return f"{protocol}://{self.nats_username}:{self.nats_password}@{rest}"
+        return self.nats_url
+
+    def get_nats_connection_options(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get NATS connection options for FastStream."""
+        options = {
+            "name": f"{self.service_name}-{self.nats_client_id}",
+            "allow_reconnect": True,
+            "reconnect_time_wait": 5,
+            "max_reconnect_attempts": -1,
+            "connect_timeout": 10,
+            "drain_timeout": 30,
+            "ping_interval": 20,
+            "max_outstanding_pings": 5,
+        }
+
+        if self.nats_token:
+            options["token"] = self.nats_token
+
+        return options
+
+    def get_redis_connection_options(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get Redis connection options with SSL support."""
+        options = {
+            "host": self.redis_host,
+            "port": self.redis_port,
+            "db": self.redis_db,
+            "decode_responses": True,
+            "socket_connect_timeout": 5,
+            "socket_timeout": 5,
+            "retry_on_timeout": True,
+        }
+
+        if self.redis_username:
+            options["username"] = self.redis_username
+
+        if self.redis_password:
+            options["password"] = self.redis_password
+
+        if self.redis_ssl:
+            options["ssl"] = True
+            options["ssl_check_hostname"] = False
+            options["ssl_cert_reqs"] = None
+
+        return options
+
+    def validate(self) -> bool:
+        """
+        Validate settings configuration.
+
+        Returns:
+            bool: True if valid, raises ValueError if invalid
+        """
+        # Check required fields based on environment
+        if self.environment == "production":
+            if not self.mongodb_password:
+                raise ValueError("MongoDB password is required in production")
+            if not (self.nats_password or self.nats_token):
+                raise ValueError("NATS authentication is required in production")
+
+        return True
+
+
+@lru_cache()
+def get_base_settings() -> DNAIBaseSettings:
+    """Get singleton base settings instance."""
+    return DNAIBaseSettings()

new file mode 100644
index 0000000..6ead485
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/config/src/config/utils.py
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+
+from pathlib import Path
+
+
+def find_env_file() -> str:
+    """
+    Find .env file by traversing up from current file location.
+
+    Searches up to 10 levels from the config package directory to find .env file.
+    Falls back to current working directory if not found.
+
+    Returns:
+        str: Absolute path to .env file
+    """
+    current = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
+
+    # Traverse up max 10 levels to find .env
+    for _ in range(10):
+        env_path = current / ".env"
+        if env_path.exists():
+            return str(env_path)
+
+        # Stop at filesystem root
+        parent = current.parent
+        if parent == current:
+            break
+        current = parent
+
+    # Fallback to .env in current working directory
+    return ".env"

diff --git a/packages/models/src/models/url_validators.py b/packages/models/src/models/url_validators.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..4f2d680
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/models/src/models/url_validators.py
@@ -0,0 +1,107 @@
+"""
+Shared URL validators for Pydantic models.
+
+Provides reusable file URL validation to avoid code duplication.
+Used by OCR, Counseling, and Audio Scoring models.
+"""
+
+from typing import List, Optional
+
+
+def validate_file_url(
+    v: str,
+    allow_local: bool = True,
+    allowed_extensions: Optional[List[str]] = None
+) -> str:
+    """
+    Validate file URL format with configurable options.
+
+    Args:
+        v: File URL or path to validate
+        allow_local: Whether to allow local file paths (/, ./, ../, file://)
+        allowed_extensions: Optional list of allowed file extensions (e.g., ['.mp3', '.mp4'])
+
+    Returns:
+        str: Validated file URL
+
+    Raises:
+        ValueError: If URL format or extension is invalid
+
+    Examples:
+        >>> # URL only (Audio Scoring)
+        >>> validate_file_url("https://example.com/audio.mp3", allow_local=False, allowed_extensions=['.mp3', '.mp4'])
+
+        >>> # URL + local paths (OCR, Counseling)
+        >>> validate_file_url("/path/to/file.pdf", allow_local=True)
+    """
+    # Base allowed URL schemes
+    allowed_schemes = ('http://', 'https://', 's3://')
+
+    # Add local path schemes if allowed
+    if allow_local:
+        allowed_schemes += ('file://', '/', './', '../')
+
+    # Validate URL/path starts with allowed scheme
+    if not v.startswith(allowed_schemes):
+        if allow_local:
+            # Also allow paths containing '/' for local files
+            if '/' not in v:
+                raise ValueError(
+                    f"File must be a valid URL or local path. "
+                    f"Allowed: {', '.join(allowed_schemes)}"
+                )
+        else:
+            raise ValueError(
+                f"File must be a valid URL starting with: {', '.join(allowed_schemes[:3])}"
+            )
+
+    # Validate file extension if specified
+    if allowed_extensions:
+        v_lower = v.lower()
+        if not any(v_lower.endswith(ext.lower()) for ext in allowed_extensions):
+            raise ValueError(
+                f"File must have one of these extensions: {', '.join(allowed_extensions)}"
+            )
+
+    return v
+
+
+def validate_audio_file_url(v: str) -> str:
+    """
+    Validate audio file URL (MP3/MP4 only, no local paths).
+
+    Used by: Audio Scoring Service
+
+    Args:
+        v: Audio file URL to validate
+
+    Returns:
+        str: Validated audio URL
+
+    Raises:
+        ValueError: If URL is invalid or extension is not MP3/MP4
+    """
+    return validate_file_url(
+        v,
+        allow_local=False,
+        allowed_extensions=['.mp3', '.mp4']
+    )
+
+
+def validate_document_file_url(v: str, allow_local: bool = True) -> str:
+    """
+    Validate document file URL (for OCR/Counseling services).
+
+    Used by: OCR Service, Counseling Service
+
+    Args:
+        v: Document file URL or path to validate
+        allow_local: Whether to allow local file paths
+
+    Returns:
+        str: Validated document URL/path
+
+    Raises:
+        ValueError: If URL format is invalid
+    """
+    return validate_file_url(v, allow_local=allow_local)

@@ -0,0 +1,1270 @@
+# 🔧 DNAISettings Refactoring Plan
+
+**Created:** 2025-12-15
+**Status:** APPROVED - Ready for implementation
+**Effort:** 6-8 hours
+**Priority:** CRITICAL
+
+---
+
+## 🎯 OBJECTIVES
+
+Refactor massive `DNAISettings` class (843 lines) to follow SOLID principles:
+
+1. **Single Responsibility** - Split into focused settings classes
+2. **Open/Closed** - Easy to add new services without modifying base classes
+3. **Inheritance-based** - Base settings in package, service-specific extensions in apps
+4. **Maintain backward compatibility** - Existing code continues to work
+5. **Support `.env` files** - Both shared (root) and service-specific
+
+---
+
+## 📊 CURRENT STATE ANALYSIS
+
+### Current Structure (843 lines)
+```
+DNAISettings (ONE massive class)
+├── MongoDB config (60+ lines)
+├── NATS config (40+ lines)
+├── Redis config (40+ lines)
+├── AWS config (30+ lines)
+├── LLM config (60+ lines)
+├── OCR config (50+ lines)
+├── SOAP config (40+ lines)
+├── Transcription config (40+ lines)
+├── Counseling-specific config (60+ lines)
+├── Audio scoring-specific config (30+ lines)
+├── Logging/Monitoring (50+ lines)
+├── Performance config (40+ lines)
+├── Validators (100+ lines)
+├── Utility methods (150+ lines)
+└── Backward compatibility (50+ lines)
+```
+
+### Problems
+❌ **Violations:**
+- Single Responsibility - manages ALL services config
+- Open/Closed - adding new service = modify this class
+- Hard to test - must mock 100+ env vars
+- Service coupling - counseling config in shared package
+
+❌ **Maintainability:**
+- 843 lines in single file
+- Mixed concerns (generic + service-specific)
+- Difficult to understand what's needed for each service
+
+❌ **Deployment:**
+- All services load all config (wasteful)
+- No clear separation of concerns
+
+---
+
+## 🏗️ TARGET ARCHITECTURE (Ý tưởng của bạn)
+
+### Inheritance-Based Multi-Layer Architecture
+
+```
+packages/config/src/config/
+├── base_settings.py                  # Core infrastructure (NEW)
+│   └── DNAIBaseSettings              # MongoDB, NATS, Redis, Logging
+│
+├── shared_settings.py                # Shared capabilities (NEW)
+│   ├── MongoDBSettings               # Nested settings class
+│   ├── NATSSettings                  # Nested settings class
+│   ├── RedisSettings                 # Nested settings class
+│   ├── AWSSettings                   # Nested settings class
+│   └── LoggingSettings               # Nested settings class
+│
+├── ai_settings.py                    # AI/LLM capabilities (NEW)
+│   └── AISettings (mixin)            # OpenAI, Cohere, Gemini
+│
+└── settings.py                       # Backward compatibility
+    └── DNAISettings = DNAIBaseSettings  # Alias for compatibility
+
+apps/<service>/settings.py            # Service-specific (NEW for each)
+└── <Service>Settings(DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings)
+    └── Service-specific fields only
+```
+
+### Key Design Decisions
+
+1. **Base in Package, Extensions in Apps**
+   - ✅ `DNAIBaseSettings` provides core infrastructure (MongoDB, NATS, Redis)
+   - ✅ Each service extends with service-specific config
+   - ✅ Services import only what they need
+
+2. **Nested Settings Classes (Composition)**
+   - ✅ `MongoDBSettings`, `NATSSettings`, etc. as separate classes
+   - ✅ Composed into `DNAIBaseSettings`
+   - ✅ Better organization, easier testing
+
+3. **Mixin Pattern for Optional Features**
+   - ✅ `AISettings` mixin for services needing LLM
+   - ✅ `OCRSettings` mixin for OCR capabilities
+   - ✅ Services pick what they need
+
+4. **Environment Variable Hierarchy**
+   - ✅ Root `.env` for shared config (MongoDB, NATS, Redis)
+   - ✅ Service `.env` for service-specific (optional)
+   - ✅ Pydantic handles merging automatically
+
+---
+
+## 📁 DETAILED FILE STRUCTURE
+
+### 1. Core Infrastructure - `packages/config/src/config/base_settings.py` (~200 lines)
+
+```python
+"""
+Core infrastructure settings shared by ALL services.
+
+Contains ONLY truly shared infrastructure:
+- MongoDB connection
+- NATS messaging
+- Redis caching
+- Logging/Monitoring
+- Performance settings
+
+NO service-specific config (OCR, LLM, Transcription, etc.)
+"""
+
+from pydantic import Field
+from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
+from typing import Dict, Any, Optional
+
+from .shared_settings import (
+    MongoDBSettings,
+    NATSSettings,
+    RedisSettings,
+    LoggingSettings,
+    PerformanceSettings
+)
+
+
+class DNAIBaseSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    Base settings for all DNAI microservices.
+
+    Provides core infrastructure settings:
+    - MongoDB database connection
+    - NATS messaging system
+    - Redis caching (optional)
+    - Logging and monitoring
+    - Performance/circuit breaker config
+
+    Usage:
+        # For generic services (no AI, no OCR)
+        from config import DNAIBaseSettings
+        settings = DNAIBaseSettings()
+
+        # For services needing AI
+        from config import DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings
+        class MyServiceSettings(DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings):
+            # Service-specific config
+            pass
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
+        env_file=".env",
+        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
+        case_sensitive=False,
+        extra="ignore",
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # CORE INFRASTRUCTURE (Nested Settings)
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    mongodb: MongoDBSettings = Field(
+        default_factory=MongoDBSettings,
+        description="MongoDB configuration"
+    )
+
+    nats: NATSSettings = Field(
+        default_factory=NATSSettings,
+        description="NATS messaging configuration"
+    )
+
+    redis: RedisSettings = Field(
+        default_factory=RedisSettings,
+        description="Redis caching configuration"
+    )
+
+    logging: LoggingSettings = Field(
+        default_factory=LoggingSettings,
+        description="Logging configuration"
+    )
+
+    performance: PerformanceSettings = Field(
+        default_factory=PerformanceSettings,
+        description="Performance and circuit breaker settings"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # SERVICE IDENTIFICATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    service_name: str = Field(
+        default="dnai_service",
+        description="Service name (override in each service)"
+    )
+
+    environment: str = Field(
+        default="development",
+        description="Environment (development, staging, production)"
+    )
+
+    debug: bool = Field(
+        default=True,
+        description="Debug mode"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # UTILITY METHODS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    def is_development(self) -> bool:
+        """Check if running in development mode."""
+        return self.environment == "development" or self.debug
+
+    def is_production(self) -> bool:
+        """Check if running in production mode."""
+        return self.environment == "production"
+
+    def is_testing(self) -> bool:
+        """Check if running in testing mode."""
+        return self.environment == "testing"
+
+
+# Singleton pattern
+_settings_instance: Optional[DNAIBaseSettings] = None
+
+
+def get_settings() -> DNAIBaseSettings:
+    """Get or create global settings instance."""
+    global _settings_instance
+    if _settings_instance is None:
+        _settings_instance = DNAIBaseSettings()
+    return _settings_instance
+```
+
+---
+
+### 2. Nested Settings Classes - `packages/config/src/config/shared_settings.py` (~300 lines)
+
+```python
+"""
+Nested settings classes for infrastructure components.
+
+Each class manages configuration for ONE infrastructure component:
+- MongoDBSettings: Database connection
+- NATSSettings: Messaging system
+- RedisSettings: Cache configuration
+- LoggingSettings: Logging setup
+- PerformanceSettings: Timeouts, retries, circuit breaker
+
+Follows Single Responsibility Principle.
+"""
+
+from pydantic import Field, field_validator
+from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
+from typing import Dict, Any, Optional
+
+
+class MongoDBSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    MongoDB database configuration.
+
+    Single Responsibility: ONLY MongoDB connection settings.
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix="MONGODB_")
+
+    # Connection
+    uri: str = Field(
+        default="mongodb://localhost:27017",
+        description="MongoDB connection URI"
+    )
+    database: str = Field(
+        default="dev_dnai_db",
+        description="Database name"
+    )
+    username: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="Username")
+    password: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="Password")
+
+    # Connection Pool
+    max_pool: int = Field(default=20, ge=1, le=100, description="Max pool size")
+    min_pool: int = Field(default=5, ge=1, le=50, description="Min pool size")
+    max_idle_time_ms: int = Field(default=20000, ge=1000, description="Max idle time (ms)")
+    server_selection_timeout_ms: int = Field(default=5000, ge=1000, description="Server selection timeout (ms)")
+    connect_timeout_ms: int = Field(default=10000, ge=1000, description="Connect timeout (ms)")
+    socket_timeout: int = Field(default=20000, ge=1000, description="Socket timeout (ms)")
+
+    @field_validator('uri')
+    @classmethod
+    def validate_uri(cls, v: str) -> str:
+        """Validate MongoDB URI format."""
+        if not v.startswith(('mongodb://', 'mongodb+srv://')):
+            raise ValueError("MongoDB URI must start with mongodb:// or mongodb+srv://")
+        return v
+
+    def get_connection_string(self) -> str:
+        """Get MongoDB connection string with authentication."""
+        if self.username and self.password:
+            if self.uri.startswith("mongodb://"):
+                return self.uri.replace(
+                    "mongodb://",
+                    f"mongodb://{self.username}:{self.password}@"
+                )
+            elif self.uri.startswith("mongodb+srv://"):
+                return self.uri  # Atlas URI already includes credentials
+        return self.uri
+
+    def get_connection_options(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get MongoDB connection options."""
+        return {
+            "maxPoolSize": self.max_pool,
+            "minPoolSize": self.min_pool,
+            "maxIdleTimeMS": self.max_idle_time_ms,
+            "serverSelectionTimeoutMS": self.server_selection_timeout_ms,
+            "connectTimeoutMS": self.connect_timeout_ms,
+            "socketTimeoutMS": self.socket_timeout,
+            "retryWrites": False,
+            "retryReads": False,
+        }
+
+
+class NATSSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    NATS messaging system configuration.
+
+    Single Responsibility: ONLY NATS connection settings.
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix="NATS_")
+
+    # Connection
+    url: str = Field(
+        default="nats://localhost:4222",
+        description="NATS server URL"
+    )
+    username: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="NATS username")
+    password: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="NATS password")
+    token: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="NATS token")
+
+    # JetStream
+    stream_replicas: int = Field(
+        default=1,
+        ge=1,
+        le=5,
+        description="Number of replicas for JetStream streams"
+    )
+
+    # Client
+    client_id: str = Field(
+        default="dnai_client",
+        description="NATS client ID"
+    )
+
+    @field_validator('url')
+    @classmethod
+    def validate_url(cls, v: str) -> str:
+        """Validate NATS URL format."""
+        if not v.startswith(('nats://', 'tls://', 'ws://', 'wss://')):
+            raise ValueError("NATS URL must start with nats://, tls://, ws://, or wss://")
+        return v
+
+    def get_authenticated_url(self) -> str:
+        """Get NATS URL with authentication credentials embedded."""
+        if self.username and self.password:
+            if "://" in self.url:
+                protocol, rest = self.url.split("://", 1)
+                return f"{protocol}://{self.username}:{self.password}@{rest}"
+        return self.url
+
+    def get_connection_options(self, service_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get NATS connection options for FastStream."""
+        options = {
+            "name": f"{service_name}-{self.client_id}",
+            "allow_reconnect": True,
+            "reconnect_time_wait": 5,
+            "max_reconnect_attempts": -1,
+            "connect_timeout": 10,
+            "drain_timeout": 30,
+            "ping_interval": 20,
+            "max_outstanding_pings": 5,
+        }
+
+        if self.token:
+            options["token"] = self.token
+
+        return options
+
+
+class RedisSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    Redis caching configuration.
+
+    Single Responsibility: ONLY Redis connection settings.
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix="REDIS_")
+
+    # Connection
+    host: str = Field(default="localhost", description="Redis host")
+    port: int = Field(default=6379, ge=1, le=65535, description="Redis port")
+    db: int = Field(default=0, ge=0, le=15, description="Redis database")
+    username: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="Redis username")
+    password: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="Redis password")
+
+    # Features
+    enabled: bool = Field(default=False, description="Enable Redis cache")
+    ssl: bool = Field(default=False, description="Enable SSL")
+
+    # Cache Settings
+    cache_prefix: str = Field(default="dnai", description="Cache key prefix")
+    cache_ttl: int = Field(default=3600, ge=60, description="Cache TTL (seconds)")
+    cache_max_size: int = Field(default=1000, description="Max cache entries")
+
+    def get_connection_options(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get Redis connection options."""
+        options = {
+            "host": self.host,
+            "port": self.port,
+            "db": self.db,
+            "decode_responses": True,
+            "socket_connect_timeout": 5,
+            "socket_timeout": 5,
+            "retry_on_timeout": True,
+        }
+
+        if self.username:
+            options["username"] = self.username
+        if self.password:
+            options["password"] = self.password
+        if self.ssl:
+            options["ssl"] = True
+            options["ssl_check_hostname"] = False
+            options["ssl_cert_reqs"] = None
+
+        return options
+
+
+class LoggingSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    Logging configuration.
+
+    Single Responsibility: ONLY logging setup.
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix="LOG_")
+
+    level: str = Field(default="INFO", description="Log level")
+    format: str = Field(default="json", description="Log format (json, text)")
+    file_path: str = Field(default="logs/", description="Log file path")
+
+    @field_validator('level')
+    @classmethod
+    def validate_level(cls, v: str) -> str:
+        """Validate log level."""
+        valid_levels = ["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL"]
+        if v.upper() not in valid_levels:
+            raise ValueError(f"Log level must be one of: {valid_levels}")
+        return v.upper()
+
+
+class PerformanceSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    Performance and reliability configuration.
+
+    Single Responsibility: ONLY performance settings.
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix="")
+
+    # Concurrency
+    max_concurrent_tasks: int = Field(
+        default=50,
+        ge=1,
+        le=1000,
+        description="Maximum concurrent tasks"
+    )
+
+    # Timeouts
+    task_timeout: int = Field(
+        default=300,
+        ge=30,
+        le=3600,
+        description="Task timeout (seconds)"
+    )
+
+    # Retries
+    retry_attempts: int = Field(
+        default=3,
+        ge=0,
+        le=10,
+        description="Retry attempts"
+    )
+    retry_delay: int = Field(
+        default=5,
+        ge=1,
+        le=60,
+        description="Retry delay (seconds)"
+    )
+
+    # Circuit Breaker
+    circuit_breaker_failure_threshold: int = Field(
+        default=5,
+        ge=1,
+        le=20,
+        description="Circuit breaker failure threshold"
+    )
+    circuit_breaker_recovery_timeout: int = Field(
+        default=60,
+        ge=10,
+        le=300,
+        description="Circuit breaker recovery timeout"
+    )
+```

+"""
+AI/LLM capabilities mixin.
+
+Services needing AI features (OpenAI, Cohere, Gemini) can inherit from AISettings.
+
+Usage:
+    class CounselingSettings(DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings):
+        pass
+"""
+
+from pydantic import Field, field_validator
+from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
+from typing import Optional
+
+
+class AISettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    AI/LLM provider configuration mixin.
+
+    Services inherit this to get AI capabilities.
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix="")
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # LLM PROVIDER SELECTION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    llm_provider: str = Field(
+        default="openai",
+        description="LLM provider (openai, cohere)"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # API KEYS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    openai_api_key: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="OpenAI API key"
+    )
+
+    cohere_api_key: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="Cohere API key"
+    )
+
+    gemini_api_key: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="Google Gemini API key"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # MODEL CONFIGURATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    openai_model: str = Field(
+        default="gpt-4.1",
+        description="OpenAI model name"
+    )
+
+    cohere_model: str = Field(
+        default="command-r-plus",
+        description="Cohere model name"
+    )
+
+    gemini_model: str = Field(
+        default="gemini-2.5-flash",
+        description="Gemini model name"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # LLM PARAMETERS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    llm_max_tokens: int = Field(
+        default=4000,
+        ge=100,
+        le=100000,
+        description="Maximum LLM tokens"
+    )
+
+    llm_temperature: float = Field(
+        default=0.1,
+        ge=0.0,
+        le=2.0,
+        description="LLM temperature"
+    )
+
+    llm_timeout: int = Field(
+        default=60,
+        ge=10,
+        le=300,
+        description="LLM request timeout (seconds)"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # VALIDATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    @field_validator('llm_provider')
+    @classmethod
+    def validate_provider(cls, v: str) -> str:
+        """Validate LLM provider."""
+        valid = ["openai", "cohere", "gemini"]
+        if v.lower() not in valid:
+            raise ValueError(f"LLM provider must be one of: {valid}")
+        return v.lower()

+#### 3b. AWS Mixin - `aws_settings.py` (~100 lines)
+
+```python
+"""
+AWS infrastructure capabilities mixin.
+
+Services using AWS (S3, Transcribe, etc.) can inherit from AWSSettings.
+
+Usage:
+    class AudioScoringSettings(DNAIBaseSettings, AWSSettings, AISettings):
+        # Audio Scoring uses AWS (Transcribe, S3) AND OpenAI (scoring, speaker roles)
+        pass
+"""
+
+from pydantic import Field
+from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
+from typing import Dict, Any, Optional
+
+
+class AWSSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """
+    AWS infrastructure configuration mixin.
+
+    Services inherit this to get AWS capabilities (S3, Transcribe, etc.).
+
+    Used by:
+    - Audio Scoring Service (S3 + Transcribe)
+    - OCR Service (S3 for file storage)
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix="AWS_")
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # CREDENTIALS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    access_key_id: str = Field(
+        default="",
+        description="AWS access key ID"
+    )
+
+    secret_access_key: str = Field(
+        default="",
+        description="AWS secret access key"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # CONFIGURATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    region: str = Field(
+        default="us-east-1",
+        description="AWS region"
+    )
+
+    bucket_name: str = Field(
+        default="dna-insight-internal",
+        description="S3 bucket name for file storage"
+    )
+
+    s3_endpoint_url: Optional[str] = Field(
+        default=None,
+        description="Custom S3 endpoint URL (for MinIO, LocalStack testing)"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # UTILITY METHODS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    def get_boto3_config(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Get boto3 client configuration.
+
+        Returns dict for boto3.client() initialization.
+        """
+        config = {
+            "aws_access_key_id": self.access_key_id,
+            "aws_secret_access_key": self.secret_access_key,
+            "region_name": self.region,
+        }
+
+        if self.s3_endpoint_url:
+            config["endpoint_url"] = self.s3_endpoint_url
+
+        return config
+
+    def get_s3_client_config(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get S3-specific client configuration."""
+        return self.get_boto3_config()
+
+    def get_transcribe_client_config(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """Get Transcribe-specific client configuration."""
+        return self.get_boto3_config()
+```

+### 4. Service-Specific Settings (Example: Counseling Service)

+```python
+"""
+Counseling Service Settings.
+
+Extends DNAIBaseSettings with counseling-specific configuration.
+"""
+
+from pydantic import Field
+from config import DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings
+
+
+class CounselingSettings(DNAIBaseSettings, AISettings):
+    """
+    Counseling service configuration.
+
+    Inherits:
+    - DNAIBaseSettings: MongoDB, NATS, Redis, Logging
+    - AISettings: OpenAI, Cohere, Gemini
+
+    Adds:
+    - Counseling-specific config
+    - OCR configuration
+    - Prompt management
+    """
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
+        env_file=[
+            ".env",                    # Root shared config
+            "apps/counseling_service/.env",  # Service-specific config (optional)
+        ],
+        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
+        case_sensitive=False,
+        extra="ignore",
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # SERVICE IDENTIFICATION (Override from base)
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    service_name: str = Field(
+        default="counseling_service",
+        description="Service name"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # OCR CONFIGURATION
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    ocr_method: str = Field(
+        default="docling",
+        description="OCR method (docling, gemini)"
+    )
+
+    ocr_max_file_size: int = Field(
+        default=10485760,  # 10MB
+        ge=1024,
+        description="Max OCR file size (bytes)"
+    )
+
+    ocr_timeout: int = Field(
+        default=180,
+        ge=30,
+        le=600,
+        description="OCR timeout (seconds)"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # NATS SUBJECTS (Counseling-specific)
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    counseling_start_subject: str = Field(
+        default="counseling.start",
+        description="Counseling start subject"
+    )
+
+    ocr_request_subject: str = Field(
+        default="ocr.request",
+        description="OCR request subject"
+    )
+
+    webhook_notification_subject: str = Field(
+        default="webhook.broadcast",
+        description="Webhook notification subject"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # PROMPT MANAGEMENT
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    enable_database_prompts: bool = Field(
+        default=True,
+        description="Enable database-driven prompts with versioning"
+    )
+
+    # =============================================================================
+    # UTILITY METHODS
+    # =============================================================================
+
+    def get_subjects(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
+        """Get NATS subjects configuration."""
+        return {
+            "counseling_start": self.counseling_start_subject,
+            "ocr_request": self.ocr_request_subject,
+            "webhook_notification": self.webhook_notification_subject,
+        }
+
+
+# Singleton
+_counseling_settings: Optional[CounselingSettings] = None
+
+
+def get_counseling_settings() -> CounselingSettings:
+    """Get counseling settings instance."""
+    global _counseling_settings
+    if _counseling_settings is None:
+        _counseling_settings = CounselingSettings()
+    return _counseling_settings
+```

+### 7. Pydantic Settings Configuration
+
+**IMPORTANT: This project uses `pydantic-settings` (installed via `pip install pydantic-settings`).**
+
+#### Correct Import Patterns
+
+**Base Settings Classes** (in `packages/config/`):
+```python
+# ✅ GOOD - Base settings import from pydantic_settings
+from pydantic import Field
+from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
+
+class DNAIBaseSettings(BaseSettings):
+    """Base configuration class."""
+
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
+        env_file=".env",
+        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
+        case_sensitive=False,
+        extra="allow"
+    )
+
+    service_name: str = Field(...)
+
+# ❌ BAD - Wrong import source
+from pydantic import Field, SettingsConfigDict  # SettingsConfigDict NOT in pydantic!
+```
+
+**Service Settings Classes** (in `apps/*/settings.py`):
+```python
+# ✅ GOOD - Service settings inherit configuration
+from functools import lru_cache
+from pydantic import Field  # Only Field needed
+from config import DNAIBaseSettings, AWSSettings, AISettings
+
+class AudioScoringSettings(DNAIBaseSettings, AWSSettings, AISettings):
+    """
+    Service configuration inheriting from base settings.
+
+    Inherits model_config from base classes - no need to import SettingsConfigDict.
+    """
+    service_name: str = Field(default="audio_scoring_service")
+
+@lru_cache()
+def get_settings() -> AudioScoringSettings:
+    return AudioScoringSettings()
+
+# ❌ BAD - Unnecessary import
+from pydantic import Field, SettingsConfigDict  # Don't need SettingsConfigDict here!
+from pydantic_settings import SettingsConfigDict  # Don't need this either!
+```
+
+#### Settings Architecture
+
+**Three-Layer Configuration:**
+
+1. **Base Settings** (`packages/config/src/config/base_settings.py`)
+   - Imports: `from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict`
+   - Defines: `model_config = SettingsConfigDict(...)`
+   - Provides: Core settings (MongoDB, NATS, Redis, Logging)
+
+2. **Specialized Settings** (`packages/config/src/config/{ai,aws}_settings.py`)
+   - Imports: `from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict`
+   - Defines: `model_config = SettingsConfigDict(...)`
+   - Provides: Domain-specific settings (AI, AWS)
+
+3. **Service Settings** (`apps/*/settings.py`)
+   - Imports: `from pydantic import Field` only
+   - Inherits: `model_config` from base classes via MRO
+   - Provides: Service-specific overrides
+
+```python
+# Configuration inheritance chain
+AudioScoringSettings
+  ├─ DNAIBaseSettings (model_config defined)
+  ├─ AWSSettings (model_config defined)
+  └─ AISettings (model_config defined)
+
+# Pydantic merges all model_config via Method Resolution Order (MRO)
+```
+
+#### Key Rules
+
+1. **Import Source**
+   - `SettingsConfigDict` → from `pydantic_settings` (NOT `pydantic`)
+   - `Field` → from `pydantic`
+   - `BaseSettings` → from `pydantic_settings`
+
+2. **When to Import SettingsConfigDict**
+   - ✅ In base settings classes that define `model_config`
+   - ❌ In service settings that inherit `model_config`
+
+3. **Installation**
+   ```bash
+   pip install pydantic-settings
+   ```
+
+#### Common Mistakes
+
+```python
+# ❌ MISTAKE 1: Wrong import source
+from pydantic import SettingsConfigDict
+# ERROR: SettingsConfigDict is in pydantic_settings, not pydantic
+
+# ❌ MISTAKE 2: Unnecessary import in service settings
+from pydantic_settings import SettingsConfigDict
+class MyServiceSettings(DNAIBaseSettings):  # Already inherits model_config
+    pass
+
+# ❌ MISTAKE 3: Forgetting pydantic-settings package
+# ERROR: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pydantic_settings'
+# FIX: pip install pydantic-settings
+
+# ✅ CORRECT: Base settings pattern
+from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
+
+class DNAIBaseSettings(BaseSettings):
+    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=".env")
+
+# ✅ CORRECT: Service settings pattern
+from pydantic import Field
+from config import DNAIBaseSettings
+
+class MyServiceSettings(DNAIBaseSettings):
+    service_name: str = Field(default="my_service")
+```
+
 ## Architecture Principles
 
 ### Event-Driven Communication

@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+from functools import lru_cache
+from pydantic import Field
+from config import DNAIBaseSettings, AWSSettings, AISettings
+
+
+class AudioScoringSettings(DNAIBaseSettings, AWSSettings, AISettings):
+    """
+    Audio Scoring Service configuration.
+
+    Inherits:
+    - DNAIBaseSettings: MongoDB, NATS, Redis, Logging
+    - AWSSettings: AWS credentials, S3, Transcribe config
+    - AISettings: OpenAI API for scoring and speaker role identification
+
+    Adds:
+    - Transcription configuration
+    - Service-specific defaults
+
+    Uses AWS Transcribe for audio transcription AND OpenAI for scoring/speaker roles
+    """
+
+    # Override service name
+    service_name: str = Field(
+        default="audio_scoring_service",
+        description="Service name"
+    )
+
+    # Transcription configuration
+    transcription_provider: str = Field(
+        default="aws",
+        description="Transcription provider (aws, google, azure, mock)"
+    )
+
+    transcription_language: str = Field(
+        default="en-US",
+        description="Default transcription language"
+    )
+
+    transcription_max_wait: int = Field(
+        default=600,
+        ge=60,
+        le=3600,
+        description="Maximum wait time for transcription (seconds)"
+    )
+
+    transcription_poll_initial_interval: float = Field(
+        default=15.0,
+        ge=1.0,
+        le=60.0,
+        description="Initial polling interval for transcription status (seconds)"
+    )
+
+    transcription_poll_max_interval: float = Field(
+        default=60.0,
+        ge=10.0,
+        le=300.0,
+        description="Maximum polling interval for transcription status (seconds)"
+    )
+
+
+# Singleton instance
+@lru_cache()
+def get_settings() -> AudioScoringSettings:
+    """
+    Get singleton settings instance for Audio Scoring Service.
+
+    Uses @lru_cache to ensure settings are loaded once and cached.
+    """
+    return AudioScoringSettings()

new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1f2f258

@@ -0,0 +1,191 @@
+from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
+from utils.logger import get_logger
+
+logger = get_logger(__name__)
+
+
+class MetadataExtractor:
+    """
+    Extracts metadata from parsed gene data for counseling reports.
+
+    Responsibilities:
+    - Extract standard report metadata (negative, positive, inconclusive genes)
+    - Extract PGx report metadata (gene arrays with phenotype/genotype)
+    - Provide default metadata for error cases
+
+    Follows Single Responsibility Principle:
+    - ONLY handles metadata extraction
+    - Does NOT generate reports
+    - Does NOT enrich genes
+    """
+
+    def extract_standard_metadata(
+        self,
+        parsed_data: Dict[str, Any],
+        patient_information: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
+    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Extract metadata from standard test results (negative, positive, inconclusive).
+
+        Args:
+            parsed_data: Parsed gene data with format:
+                {
+                    "response": [[negative_genes], [positive_genes], [inconclusive_genes]],
+                    "format": "standard"
+                }
+            patient_information: Optional patient information
+
+        Returns:
+            Metadata dict with negative_genes, positive_genes, inconclusive_genes, patient_information
+
+        Example:
+            >>> parsed_data = {
+            >>>     "response": [
+            >>>         ["BRCA1", "BRCA2"],  # negative
+            >>>         ["TP53"],             # positive
+            >>>         ["ATM"]               # inconclusive
+            >>>     ]
+            >>> }
+            >>> metadata = extractor.extract_standard_metadata(parsed_data)
+            >>> # metadata = {
+            >>> #     "negative_genes": ["BRCA1", "BRCA2"],
+            >>> #     "positive_genes": ["TP53"],
+            >>> #     "inconclusive_genes": ["ATM"],
+            >>> #     "patient_information": {...}
+            >>> # }
+        """
+        response = parsed_data.get("response", [])
+
+        if not isinstance(response, list) or len(response) != 3:
+            logger.warning(f"Invalid standard format: expected 3-array, got {type(response)}")
+            return self.get_default_standard_metadata()
+
+        # Extract 3 arrays: [negative, positive, inconclusive]
+        negative_genes = response[0] if len(response) > 0 else []
+        positive_genes = response[1] if len(response) > 1 else []
+        inconclusive_genes = response[2] if len(response) > 2 else []
+
+        metadata = {
+            "negative_genes": negative_genes,
+            "positive_genes": positive_genes,
+            "inconclusive_genes": inconclusive_genes,
+            "patient_information": patient_information or {}
+        }
+
+        logger.info(
+            f"✅ Extracted standard metadata: "
+            f"{len(negative_genes)} negative, "
+            f"{len(positive_genes)} positive, "
+            f"{len(inconclusive_genes)} inconclusive"
+        )
+
+        return metadata
+
+    async def extract_pgx_metadata(
+        self,
+        parsed_data: Dict[str, Any],
+        patient_information: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
+        gene_enricher=None
+    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Extract metadata from PGx test results.
+
+        Args:
+            parsed_data: Parsed gene data with format:
+                {
+                    "response": [
+                        {"symbol": "CYP2D6", "result": "poor metabolizer", "phenotype": "...", "status": "positive"},
+                        ...
+                    ],
+                    "format": "pgx"
+                }
+            patient_information: Optional patient information
+            gene_enricher: Optional PGxGeneEnricher for enriching genes with database data
+
+        Returns:
+            Metadata dict with genes array and patient_information
+
+        Example:
+            >>> parsed_data = {
+            >>>     "response": [
+            >>>         {"symbol": "CYP2D6", "result": "poor metabolizer", "phenotype": "*1/*4", "status": "positive"}
+            >>>     ]
+            >>> }
+            >>> metadata = await extractor.extract_pgx_metadata(parsed_data, gene_enricher=enricher)
+            >>> # metadata = {
+            >>> #     "genes": [
+            >>> #         {
+            >>> #             "symbol": "CYP2D6",
+            >>> #             "result": "poor metabolizer",
+            >>> #             "phenotype": "*1/*4",
+            >>> #             "genotype": "*1/*4",  # Added from enrichment
+            >>> #             "description": "...",  # Added from enrichment
+            >>> #             "affected_drugs": [...],  # Added from enrichment
+            >>> #             "clinical_significance": "...",  # Added from enrichment
+            >>> #             "status": "positive"
+            >>> #         }
+            >>> #     ],
+            >>> #     "patient_information": {...}
+            >>> # }
+        """
+        response = parsed_data.get("response", [])
+
+        if not isinstance(response, list):
+            logger.warning(f"Invalid PGx format: expected array, got {type(response)}")
+            return self.get_default_pgx_metadata()
+
+        # Enrich genes with database data if enricher provided
+        if gene_enricher:
+            enriched_genes = await gene_enricher.enrich_genes(response)
+        else:
+            enriched_genes = response
+
+        metadata = {
+            "genes": enriched_genes,
+            "patient_information": patient_information or {}
+        }
+
+        logger.info(f"✅ Extracted PGx metadata: {len(enriched_genes)} genes")
+
+        return metadata
+
+    def get_default_standard_metadata(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Get default metadata for standard tests when extraction fails.
+
+        Returns:
+            Default metadata with empty gene arrays
+        """
+        return {
+            "negative_genes": [],
+            "positive_genes": [],
+            "inconclusive_genes": [],
+            "patient_information": {}
+        }
+
+    def get_default_pgx_metadata(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Get default metadata for PGx tests when extraction fails.
+
+        Returns:
+            Default metadata with empty genes array
+        """
+        return {
+            "genes": [],
+            "patient_information": {}
+        }
+
+    def get_default_metadata(self, test_type: str = "general") -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Get default metadata based on test type.
+
+        Args:
+            test_type: Test type (standard, pgx, immunodeficiency, neurocognitive)
+
+        Returns:
+            Default metadata for the test type
+        """
+        if test_type.lower() == "pgx":
+            return self.get_default_pgx_metadata()
+        else:
+            return self.get_default_standard_metadata()
diff --git a/apps/counseling_service/core/engines/pgx_gene_enricher.py b/apps/counseling_service/core/engines/pgx_gene_enricher.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..d236167
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/counseling_service/core/engines/pgx_gene_enricher.py
@@ -0,0 +1,189 @@
+import asyncio
+from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
+from infrastructure.repositories.pgx_repository import MongoPGxRepository
+from utils.logger import get_logger
+
+logger = get_logger(__name__)
+
+
+class PGxGeneEnricher:
+    """
+    Enriches PGx genes with database information.
+
+    Responsibilities:
+    - Enrich genes with genotype, phenotype, affected drugs
+    - Handle synonym search for gene symbols
+    - Merge database data with parsed data
+    - Provide defaults for missing genes
+
+    Follows Single Responsibility Principle:
+    - ONLY handles gene enrichment
+    - Does NOT extract metadata
+    - Does NOT generate reports
+    """
+
+    def __init__(self, pgx_repository: MongoPGxRepository):
+        """
+        Initialize PGx gene enricher.
+
+        Args:
+            pgx_repository: MongoDB repository for PGx data
+        """
+        if not pgx_repository:
+            raise ValueError("pgx_repository is required")
+
+        self.pgx_repository = pgx_repository
+        logger.info("✅ PGxGeneEnricher initialized")
+
+    async def enrich_genes(self, genes: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
+        """
+        Enrich all genes with database information asynchronously.
+
+        Performs parallel database lookups for all genes for better performance.
+
+        Args:
+            genes: List of gene dicts with at minimum {"symbol": "..."}
+
+        Returns:
+            List of enriched gene dicts with additional fields:
+            - genotype: Gene genotype
+            - phenotype: Gene phenotype
+            - description: Gene description
+            - affected_drugs: List of affected drugs
+            - clinical_significance: Clinical significance
+
+        Example:
+            >>> genes = [
+            >>>     {"symbol": "CYP2D6", "result": "poor metabolizer", "status": "positive"},
+            >>>     {"symbol": "CYP2C19", "result": "intermediate metabolizer", "status": "positive"}
+            >>> ]
+            >>> enriched = await enricher.enrich_genes(genes)
+            >>> # enriched[0] now has genotype, phenotype, affected_drugs, etc.
+        """
+        if not genes:
+            return []
+
+        logger.info(f"🔄 Enriching {len(genes)} PGx genes with database data...")
+
+        # Parallel lookup for all genes
+        tasks = [self._enrich_single_gene(gene) for gene in genes]
+        enriched_genes = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
+
+        successful_enrichments = sum(1 for g in enriched_genes if "genotype" in g)
+        logger.info(
+            f"✅ Enriched {successful_enrichments}/{len(genes)} genes "
+            f"({len(genes) - successful_enrichments} used defaults)"
+        )
+
+        return enriched_genes
+
+    async def _enrich_single_gene(self, gene: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Enrich a single gene with database information.
+
+        Args:
+            gene: Gene dict with at minimum {"symbol": "..."}
+
+        Returns:
+            Enriched gene dict (or original with defaults if not found)
+        """
+        gene_symbol = gene.get("symbol", "").upper()
+
+        if not gene_symbol:
+            logger.warning("Gene missing symbol, using defaults")
+            return self._get_default_gene(gene)
+
+        # Try direct lookup first
+        gene_info = await self.pgx_repository.get_gene_by_symbol(gene_symbol)
+
+        # Try synonym search if not found
+        if not gene_info:
+            gene_info = await self._try_synonym_search(gene_symbol)
+
+        # Merge database data with original gene data
+        if gene_info:
+            return self._merge_gene_data(gene, gene_info)
+        else:
+            logger.warning(f"⚠️ No database info found for {gene_symbol}, using defaults")
+            return self._get_default_gene(gene)
+
+    async def _try_synonym_search(self, gene_symbol: str) -> Optional[Dict]:
+        """
+        Try to find gene by synonym if direct lookup fails.
+
+        Args:
+            gene_symbol: Gene symbol to search
+
+        Returns:
+            Gene info dict if found via synonym, None otherwise
+        """
+        try:
+            gene_info = await self.pgx_repository.get_gene_by_synonym(gene_symbol)
+            if gene_info:
+                logger.info(f"✅ Found {gene_symbol} via synonym search")
+            return gene_info
+        except Exception as e:
+            logger.warning(f"Synonym search failed for {gene_symbol}: {e}")
+            return None
+
+    def _merge_gene_data(
+        self,
+        original_gene: Dict[str, Any],
+        gene_info: Dict[str, Any]
+    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Merge original parsed gene with database gene info.
+
+        Database fields are added if not present in original.
+        Original fields take precedence over database fields.
+
+        Args:
+            original_gene: Original parsed gene dict
+            gene_info: Database gene info
+
+        Returns:
+            Merged gene dict
+        """
+        # Start with original gene data
+        merged = original_gene.copy()
+
+        # Add database fields if not present
+        if "genotype" not in merged:
+            merged["genotype"] = gene_info.get("genotype", "")
+
+        if "phenotype" not in merged:
+            merged["phenotype"] = gene_info.get("phenotype", "")
+
+        if "description" not in merged:
+            merged["description"] = gene_info.get("description", "")
+
+        if "affected_drugs" not in merged:
+            merged["affected_drugs"] = gene_info.get("affected_drugs", [])
+
+        if "clinical_significance" not in merged:
+            merged["clinical_significance"] = gene_info.get("clinical_significance", "")
+
+        return merged
+
+    def _get_default_gene(self, gene: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        """
+        Get default gene structure when database lookup fails.
+
+        Preserves original fields and adds empty defaults for missing fields.
+
+        Args:
+            gene: Original gene dict
+
+        Returns:
+            Gene dict with default values for missing fields
+        """
+        return {
+            "symbol": gene.get("symbol", ""),
+            "result": gene.get("result", ""),
+            "phenotype": gene.get("phenotype", ""),
+            "genotype": gene.get("genotype", ""),
+            "description": "",
+            "affected_drugs": [],
+            "clinical_significance": "",
+            "status": gene.get("status", "")
+        }
